items.title: Canada's economy saw slight growth in January
items.summary_auto: Canada's economy saw slight growth in January, as gains in goods-producing industries like mining offset a slowdown in manufacturing, Statistics Canada said on Tuesday.

items.title: U.S. Army says it's investigating military helicopter flyby at Kid Rock's house
items.summary_auto: The U.S. Army said on Monday it was investigating why military helicopters were operating near the house of singer Kid Rock, seemingly in support of the vocal backer of U.S. President Donald ‌Trump, and also near anti-Trump protests.

items.title: King Charles to make first state visit to U.S.
items.summary_auto: King Charles and Queen Camilla will make a state visit to the United States this spring, Buckingham Palace has announced.

items.title: Former Raptors president Masai Ujiri joins ownership group of WNBA's Toronto Tempo
items.summary_auto: Masai Ujiri is returning to professional basketball in Toronto. Ujiri officially joined the ownership group of the WNBA's Toronto Tempo on Tuesday after spending 12 years in the front office of the NBA's Toronto Raptors.

items.title: Robert Pattinson, Zendaya's The Drama is disturbing, controversial and fascinating
items.summary_auto: That The Drama blossoms into a subversive investigation into the limits of empathy doesn't really matter. All that will matter is the disappointment theatre-goers will feel walking out of a supposed rom-com they expected to be like Sleepless in Seattle, but that instead feels closer to Midsommar.

items.title: Weather watchers mourn the end of national Weatheradio service
items.summary_auto: There is now one less way to get weather information in Canada. Environment and Climate Change Canada is ending its long-running Weatheradio service. It comes after ECCC closed many of its weather accounts on social media specific to provinces and territories.

items.title: Why are bags of Big Mac sauce washing up along the Bay of Fundy?
items.summary_auto: Empty bags of Big Mac and McChicken sauce have been washing up along the Bay of Fundy recently. And local residents are not lovin’ it.

items.title: U.S. strikes city home to Iranian nuclear site, Netanyahu says Israel 'beyond halfway point' in its mission
items.summary_auto: The United States hit the central Iranian city of Isfahan early Tuesday, sending a massive fireball into the sky, and Tehran struck a fully loaded Kuwaiti oil tanker in the Persian Gulf.

items.title: Her scammer only got house arrest. She says Canada's sentences are a 'joke'
items.summary_auto: Scam victims feel an increasing sense that the Canadian justice system is overly lenient on the people who took advantage of them.

items.title: CTA to lower air passenger complaints fee after pressure from airlines, transport minister, documents show
items.summary_auto: Internal documents show airlines and then Transport Minister Anita Anand pressed the Canadian Transportation Agency to scale back a proposed complaints fee — raising concerns about political interference.

items.title: Public servant says she's 'collateral damage' as government prepares to test new pay system
items.summary_auto: A longtime federal public servant says she’s become "collateral damage" as her department clears its backlog of Phoenix payroll issues so it can test replacement software — and in doing so introduced an error to her file, refused to fix it and is now clawing back hundreds of dollars per paycheque.

items.title: Most people across Canada will no longer need to pay nurse practitioners for primary care
items.summary_auto: As of Wednesday, the federal government will require provinces and territories to start to pay for primary care services, including those provided by nurse practitioners. The new policy aims to ensure Canadians aren't being billed for "medically necessary services."

items.title: Heat dome burns off mountain snow in western U.S.,  flashing warning for fire season
items.summary_auto: Mountains that normally see their peak snowpack in March are brown this year, thanks to a spring heat dome that baked western U.S. for much of the second half of the month. That's raising alarm bells for the fire season that's already ramping up.

items.title: Patients of Ontario dental clinic speak out after hepatitis, HIV tests urged due to sterilization lapses
items.summary_auto: Former patients of a dental clinic in Brantford, Ont., are being warned by Grand Erie Public Health to get tested for hepatitis B, hepatitis C and HIV due to an infection prevention and control lapse investigation.

items.title: Trump is considering U.S. ground troops in Iran. Here are his options
items.summary_auto: As U.S. President Donald Trump considers ordering U.S. ground troops into Iran, military experts say trying to capture any of the most likely targets would pose a significant challenge.

items.title: Ottawa authorizes emergency use of strychnine in Alberta, Saskatchewan to address gopher infestation
items.summary_auto: Ottawa is authorizing the controlled and time-limited emergency use of strychnine to address millions of dollars worth of damage in Alberta and Saskatchewan caused by an infestation of Richardson's ground squirrels, known colloquially as gophers.

items.title: #TheMoment Banff's famous grizzly 'The Boss' came out of hibernation
items.summary_auto: Wildlife photographer Jason Leo Bantle tells The National about the moment Banff National Park's famous grizzly bear, 'The Boss,' came out of hibernation.

items.title: Toronto Maple Leafs fire general manager Brad Treliving
items.summary_auto: Maple Leaf Sports and Entertainment have decided to part ways with Toronto Maple Leafs General Manager Brad Treliving.

items.title: Alberta pushing for constitutional change to give provinces say over appointment of judges
items.summary_auto: Alberta's government is calling on Ottawa to change the Constitution to give provinces more of a say in how judges are appointed at the provincial level.

items.title: B.C. youth charged after chats with U.S. teen who allegedly made school shooting threats: police
items.summary_auto: A Sooke, B.C., youth has been arrested and charged after online chats with a U.S. teenager who allegedly made school shooting threats, RCMP say.

items.title: How provincial budget deficits impact you
items.summary_auto: Every province that has released a 2026-27 budget is projecting a deficit. But is that cause for concern? Note: P.E.I and Newfoundland and Labrador had not yet released their budgets at the time of publication.

items.title: Alberta NDP not considering name change — for now — amid distance from federal party
items.summary_auto: Despite speedy efforts to distance provincial New Democrats from the new-look federal party, Albertans shouldn't necessarily expect any changes to the provincial orange brand. But Alberta NDP Leader Naheed Nenshi told reporters Monday that if members of the party want to have a conversation around a name change, he's open to the possibility.

items.title: 40 species, including snowy owl, added to UN list for greater protections
items.summary_auto: A week of negotiations has led to 40 species added on a list of protections under the Convention on the Conservation of Migratory Species of Wild Animals, the 15th meeting of countries that are party to a UN treaty signed in 1979.

items.title: Man who crashed truck into Michigan synagogue inspired by Hezbollah, FBI says
items.summary_auto: An armed man who crashed his pickup truck into a major Detroit-area synagogue earlier in March was inspired by the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah and had sought to inflict as much damage as he could on Jewish people, the FBI said Monday.

items.title: Former investor says he's disappointed by Moose Jaw scientist Goodenowe's 'new lies and new promises'
items.summary_auto: As Dayan Goodenowe makes new claims about a potential treatment for Alzheimer's, a former investor says the controversial Moose Jaw-based scientist has a history of making unproven promises that he can't keep.

items.title: Human rights group says at least 70 killed in wake of attack in Haiti's Artibonite region
items.summary_auto: At least 70 people were killed and 30 injured during an attack near Petite-Rivière in Haiti's Artibonite region, a human rights ‌group said on Monday, numbers that were significantly higher than official estimates which put the death toll at around 16.

items.title: New bill would remove deadlines for Alberta government to act on citizen petitions
items.summary_auto: If Bill 23 passes, it would be the third time the United Conservative government has substantively amended the Citizen Initiative Act, and the second substantive set amendments to recall legislation since those two democratic options were introduced in 2021.

items.title: In our search for alien life, stars might be muddying their signals
items.summary_auto: Humans have wondered for centuries whether we are alone in the universe — and a new study suggests that if alien civilizations have been trying to communicate with us, we mostly haven’t been listening for the right signals.

items.title: Bread price-fixing settlement scammers are trying to get your dough
Human rights tribunal approves $8.5B child welfare deal for Ontario First Nations
items.summary_auto: The Canadian Human Rights Tribunal has approved a landmark First Nations child welfare deal between the federal government and First Nations in Ontario, partially ending a decades-long discrimination case.

items.title: Robert Land Academy, now-shut private school in Ontario, under police investigation for alleged child abuse
items.summary_auto: Peel Regional Police are leading an investigation into allegations of child abuse at Robert Land Academy, a military-style private school for boys in Ontario's Niagara Region that operated for decades until last year. Dozens of former students have alleged physical and emotional abuse, and in some cases, sexual exploitation.

items.title: Why Kharg Island is key for the U.S., Iran and the world's economy
items.summary_auto: Kharg Island is Iran's key oil export hub and perhaps the most important location in the war that the U.S. and Israel launched on Iran on Feb. 28.

items.title: Can Avi Lewis make the federal NDP relevant again — without undermining its provincial cousins?
items.summary_auto: Avi Lewis, a former television host, journalist and documentarian, promises to make the federal NDP relevant again. But the first question for his leadership is whether he'll do so at the expense of the NDP's provincial wings.

items.title: Céline Dion to return to concert stage with Paris residency this fall
items.summary_auto: It's Céline Dion's 58th birthday, but it's her adoring fans who may be receiving the biggest gift of all — her return to the concert stage.

items.title: Israeli parliament approves the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis
items.summary_auto: The bill's passage marked the culmination of a years-long push by Israel's far-right to escalate punishment for Palestinians convicted of nationalistic offenses against Israelis.

items.title: Carney defends Canada's approach to forced labour amid fallout from floor-crossing MP's comments
What impacts your heart health can also raise dementia risk, new Canadian guidelines say
items.summary_auto: Pay attention to the link between heart and brain health, new Canadian recommendations say.

items.title: Brain and heart health are tied together. Here are 5 ways to control both
items.summary_auto: Pay attention to the link between heart and brain health, new Canadian recommendations say.

items.title: Northern Manitoba, Sask. communities working to resolve cross-border health-care issues
items.summary_auto: People in and around Flin Flon have started a health care committee to improve regional care. Saskatchewan residents use the hospital in the northern Manitoba community, but sometimes have trouble accessing care.
